typhoon: Stay away from windows and glass
Move to safest area (interior room, lower floor not prone to flooding)
Charge phone and all power banks
Turn on radio / check official updates
flood: Move to higher ground or upper floor
Turn off electricity (if safe to do so)
earthquake: Stay away from glass and heavy objects
landslide: Move away from slopes and hillsides
fire: Identify exits and practice escape plan
If fire starts: Get out, stay out, call for help
Crawl low under smoke to avoid inhalation
volcanic_eruption: Prepare a go-bag (water, food, flashlight, meds, masks, documents)
Keep N95 mask or cloth to protect from ash
Seal windows/doors to prevent ash from entering
Evacuate immediately if within danger zone
storm_surge: Know if your area is coastal and flood-prone
Prepare evacuation plan to higher ground
Secure important belongings in waterproof bags
Evacuate early—do NOT wait for water to rise
drought: Store clean drinking water (at least 3 days supply)
Practice water conservation (limit washing, reuse water)
Avoid wasting electricity (reduces strain on resources)
heat_wave: Drink plenty of water (even if not thirsty)
Avoid outdoor activities during peak heat (10 AM-4 PM)
Stay in shaded or ventilated areas
Watch for signs of heat exhaustion (dizziness, nausea)
tsunami: Know evacuation routes to higher ground
After strong earthquake, move inland immediately
Do NOT wait for official warning if water suddenly recedes
Stay away from coastline until declared safe
